$comment: Single source of truth for the Windows development-VM toolchain that lives OUTSIDE the
repository (system-provisioned tools). In-repo, version-pinned dependencies are NOT copied
here to avoid drift: the .NET target framework and Windows SDK build live in the csproj files,
and the Windows App SDK / Windows SDK BuildTools versions live in Directory.Packages.props.
scripts/bootstrap-windows-dev-vm.ps1 installs from this manifest; scripts/report-windows-env.ps1
verifies the running VM against it and derives the in-repo versions from their real sources.
No hostnames, usernames, network addresses, licenses, or secrets belong in this file.

vmEnvelope.note: Recommended development baseline for a comfortable WinUI + Rust build/test loop. This is NOT a runtime requirement for OK Player itself; the shipped app targets ordinary Windows 11 hardware.

os.note: 22621 matches TargetPlatformMinVersion in src/OkPlayer.App/OkPlayer.App.csproj. The Windows SDK build the app compiles against (26100) is provisioned by the Visual Studio workload below, not by the OS build.

dotnetSdk.note: Matches the net9.0 / net9.0-windows target frameworks and the setup-dotnet 9.0.x pin in .github/workflows/ci.yml.

cmake.note: Only required when building libmpv/ffmpeg from source (scripts/build-local-mpv.sh path). Prebuilt natives via scripts/fetch-natives.ps1 do not need it.

ninja.note: Same scope as cmake: source native builds only.

rustup.note: The MSVC (not GNU) toolchain is required so the future okp-ffi C ABI links against the same runtime as the WinUI shell.

visualStudio.note: Build Tools is sufficient for command-line build/test/package. Community is the full IDE alternative. The bootstrap falls back to the alternativeWingetIds when the primary winget id is unavailable; minVersion 17.12.0 is the VS 2022 floor for .NET 9 / WinUI 3 workloads. The Windows11SDK.26100 component provides the SDK build the WinUI shell compiles against.

natives.note: libmpv (GPL, x86_64) and ffmpeg (GPL, win64) track their own upstream release cadence and are fetched, not version-pinned here. The report records the resolved libmpv-2.dll product version at scan time.
